summary: Re-linked Peach fighter code by moving the nonmatching ftPe_Float and ftPe_SpecialLw translation units to standalone asm while cleaning inline MUST_MATCH assembly out of the C sources. Build metadata was updated so obj_files.mk links the new asm objects, while configure.py marks several other Peach files as Matching. The PR had no review or body text, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.
what_changed: Added standalone assembly object asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.s containing Peach float routines such as ftPe_Float_CheckContinueInput, ftPe_8011BA54, ftPe_8011BAD8, ftPe_8011BB6C, ftPe_Float_Anim, ftPe_Float_IASA, ftPe_Float_Phys, and ftPe_Float_Coll.

Added standalone assembly object asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_SpecialLw.s containing Peach down-special/vegetable routines including pickVeg, ftPe_SpecialLw_UnsetVeg, ftPe_SpecialLw_8011CFA0, spawnVeg, ftPe_SpecialLw_Enter, ftPe_SpecialAirLw_Enter, animation callbacks, physics callbacks, and collision callbacks.
Removed inline MUST_MATCH asm blocks from src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.c for ftPe_8011BB6C while leaving the C implementation present.
Removed inline MUST_MATCH asm blocks from src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_SpecialLw.c for spawnVeg while leaving the C implementation present.
Removed redundant <baselib/gobj.h> includes from ftPe_Float.c, ftPe_SpecialLw.c, and ftPe_SpecialHi.c.
Updated obj_files.mk so ftPe_Float and ftPe_SpecialLw are linked from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/*.s.o instead of src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/*.c.o.

Updated configure.py Peach object status: ftPe_Init.c, ftPe_FloatAttack.c, ftPe_SpecialS.c, ftPe_SpecialHi.c, and ftPe_SpecialN.c changed from NonMatching to Matching; ftPe_Float.c and ftPe_SpecialLw.c remained NonMatching.
Added .aider* to .gitignore and removed a trailing blank line at end of .gitignore.
smart_moves: Converted problematic inline MUST_MATCH assembly into normal standalone asm files, reducing clutter in C sources while preserving exact object code for relinking.
Kept symbol names and linkage explicit in the asm with .fn global/local and .endfn annotations, making the fallback objects searchable and linkable.
Updated obj_files.mk and configure.py together, which is necessary when a translation unit is no longer compiled from C but still participates in the same runtime library link order.
Preserved small .sdata2 float constants in the asm files, avoiding accidental changes to constant placement for 0.0 and 1.0.
Used relinking to unblock adjacent Peach files from being marked Matching without forcing every Peach function in the affected units to match in C first.
decomp_lessons: If only part of a fighter translation unit is still nonmatching but the current build needs a clean relink, moving the whole nonmatching TU to standalone asm can be a pragmatic bridge while keeping the C decomp attempt in src for future work.
Do not leave large inline asm under MUST_MATCH when a standalone asm object can carry the exact code more cleanly; it improves C readability and separates matching work from link-order preservation.
When replacing a C object with an asm object, update obj_files.mk paths directly from src/.../*.c.o to asm/.../*.s.o and verify configure.py object matching states still reflect the C source status.
Standalone asm fallbacks should include .file with the original C filename, .text layout comments, .balign directives, local/global function annotations, and any required .sdata2 constants.

Peach float and down-special code touches fighter state offsets, item/vegetable state, callbacks, effects, and collision transitions, so preserving exact control flow and callback pointer writes is important during partial decomp.
naming_conventions: Peach fighter symbols use the ftPe_ prefix, for example ftPe_Float_CheckContinueInput, ftPe_SpecialLw_UnsetVeg, and ftPe_SpecialAirLw_Enter.
Named callbacks follow the fighter action suffix convention: _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
Still-unknown Peach functions retain address-style names such as ftPe_8011BA54, ftPe_8011BAD8, ftPe_8011BB6C, and ftPe_SpecialLw_8011CFA0.
Local helper symbols in these files use descriptive lower/camel-style names such as pickVeg, spawnVeg, handleAirColl, and handleColl.
Assembly labels use generated local labels of the form .L_0000002C, matching project asm style for extracted functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced object-file inputs for ftPe_Float and ftPe_SpecialLw with extracted standalone PowerPC asm to preserve matching bytes while relinking ftPeach.
Moved inline asm implementations for ftPe_8011BB6C and spawnVeg out of C, leaving ordinary C versions available for future matching attempts but not selected for object output.

Kept branch/call targets symbolic in asm, including Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftParts_8007500C, efAsync_Spawn, HSD_Randi, itGetKind, it_802BD45C, it_802BD4AC, ftCommon_8007D268, ft_800831CC, ft_8008403C, and ft_80082C74.
Preserved local helper scope in asm with .fn pickVeg, local; .fn spawnVeg, local; .fn handleAirColl, local; and .fn handleColl, local.
Used explicit .sdata2 objects for float constants named "@251"/"@252" in ftPe_Float.s and "@291"/"@292" in ftPe_SpecialLw.s.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR dump.
No PR body text was present; rationale is inferred from the title "Re-link `ftPeach`" and the build/diff changes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.s back to matching C.
Find later PRs that convert asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_SpecialLw.s back to matching C.
Search for other relink PRs that replace C objects with standalone asm in obj_files.mk.
Search Peach decomp work involving ftPe_8011BB6C, spawnVeg, pickVeg, and vegetable item kind 0x63.
Check whether .aider* ignore entries appear in nearby PRs or were introduced only here.
